Beginner.text: What is a data structure?
options: A way to organize and store data
Beginner.correct: A way to organize and store data

Beginner.text: Which of the following is a linear data structure?
What is the time complexity of accessing an element in an array by index?
Which data structure uses LIFO (Last In First Out) principle?
Which data structure uses FIFO (First In First Out) principle?
What is a linked list?
options: A linear collection of data elements whose order is not given by their physical placement
A collection of elements with index positions
Beginner.correct: A linear collection of data elements whose order is not given by their physical placement

Beginner.text: What is the maximum number of children a binary tree node can have?
Which traversal method visits the root node first, then left subtree, then right subtree?
What is a hash table?
options: A data structure that implements an associative array
Beginner.correct: A data structure that implements an associative array

Beginner.text: What does the 'push' operation do in a stack?

Intermediate.text: What is the time complexity of binary search on a sorted array?
What is a binary search tree (BST)?
options: A tree where left child is smaller than parent and right child is larger
A tree with exactly two children
A tree that is always balanced
A tree with no specific order
Intermediate.correct: A tree where left child is smaller than parent and right child is larger

Intermediate.text: What is the worst-case time complexity of quicksort?
Which data structure is used for implementing recursion?

options: A queue where elements have associated priorities
A queue that processes elements in order
Intermediate.correct: A queue where elements have associated priorities

Intermediate.text: What is the time complexity for inserting an element at the beginning of a singly linked list?

options: A non-linear data structure consisting of vertices and edges
Intermediate.correct: A non-linear data structure consisting of vertices and edges

Intermediate.text: What is the time complexity of finding an element in a hash table in the average case?

options: A tree where height difference between left and right subtrees is at most 1
A tree with equal number of nodes
A tree with all leaves at same level
Intermediate.correct: A tree where height difference between left and right subtrees is at most 1

Intermediate.text: What is the purpose of a deque (double-ended queue)?
options: Allows insertion and deletion at both ends
Intermediate.correct: Allows insertion and deletion at both ends

Advanced.text: What is the time complexity of finding the shortest path using Dijkstra's algorithm with a binary heap?
What is a B-tree used for?
What is the time complexity of Floyd-Warshall algorithm?

options: A tree with red and black nodes

Advanced.text: What is the space complexity of merge sort?
What is a suffix tree used for?
What is the amortized time complexity for inserting into a dynamic array?
What is a trie data structure?
options: A tree structure for storing strings
Advanced.correct: A tree structure for storing strings

Advanced.text: What is the time complexity of Kruskal's algorithm for MST?

options: A probabilistic data structure for efficient search
Advanced.correct: A probabilistic data structure for efficient search

options: A heap data structure with efficient decrease-key operation
A heap that stores Fibonacci numbers
Expert.correct: A heap data structure with efficient decrease-key operation

Expert.text: What is the time complexity of union-find with path compression?
options: O(α(n)) where α is the inverse Ackermann function
Expert.correct: O(α(n)) where α is the inverse Ackermann function
